Question: Is the function $$f$$ graphed in the following image one-to-one?
Find the inverse of the function $$f ( x ) = 3 x \text{/} ( x - 2 ) .$$ State the domain and range of the inverse function.
Answer: $$f^{-1} ( x ) = \frac{2 x}{x - 3} .$$ The domain of $$f^{-1}$$ is $$\{ x | x \ne 3 \} .$$ The range of $$f^{-1}$$ is $$\{ y | y \ne 2 \} .$$

Question: Sketch the graph of $$f ( x ) = 2 x + 3$$ and the graph of its inverse using the symmetry property of inverse functions.

Consider $$f ( x ) = 1 \text{/} x^{2}$$ restricted to the domain $$( \text{unicode[:-]} \infty , 0 ) .$$ Verify that $$f$$ is one-to-one on this domain. Determine the domain and range of the inverse of $$f$$ and find a formula for $$f^{-1} .$$
Answer: The domain of $$f^{-1}$$ is $$( 0 , \infty ) .$$ The range of $$f^{-1}$$ is $$( \text{unicode[:-]} \infty , 0 ) .$$ The inverse function is given by the formula $$f^{-1} ( x ) = -1 \text{/} \sqrt{x} .$$
